threat_categories.name: DDoS
threat_categories.description: Volumetric flood attacks targeting data center network infrastructure, overwhelming bandwidth and service availability.
ioc_examples: High-volume UDP/ICMP traffic from geographically diverse sources exceeding 10 Gbps
DNS amplification packets with spoofed source addresses targeting infrastructure IPs
SYN flood packets at >1M packets/second targeting TCP services
HTTP request flood from botnet ASNs at >50,000 requests/second to web-facing services

threat_categories.name: XSS
threat_categories.description: Injection attacks targeting web management consoles and SCADA HMI interfaces exposed in the IT/OT convergence zone.
ioc_examples: HTTP POST requests containing <script> tags or JavaScript event handlers in form fields
Encoded payloads: %3Cscript%3E, <script> in URL parameters or cookies
Outbound HTTP requests from HMI server to unknown external domains after user interaction
Web application log entries showing alert() or document.cookie access patterns in user inputs

threat_categories.name: ARP Spoofing
threat_categories.description: Link-layer interception attacks enabling man-in-the-middle positioning between OT devices and controllers on flat IT/OT networks.
ioc_examples: Gratuitous ARP replies from unexpected MAC addresses claiming ownership of known PLC/RTU IP addresses
Duplicate IP entries with different MAC addresses in switch MAC address tables
ARP request/reply ratio anomaly: >100 gratuitous ARPs/minute from a single host
SCADA server ARP cache entries showing MAC addresses inconsistent with documented network topology

threat_categories.name: Ransomware
threat_categories.description: File-encrypting malware targeting data center hosts, with lateral movement risk into OT historian and SCADA systems.
ioc_examples: Mass file rename events with unknown extensions (.locked, .encrypted, .crypt) at >1000 files/minute
Creation of ransom notes (README.txt, DECRYPT_INSTRUCTIONS.html) in multiple directory paths
Volume shadow copy deletion commands: vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet
Outbound HTTPS connections to Tor exit nodes or dark web onion address resolution attempts

threat_categories.name: ICS Anomaly
threat_categories.description: Deviations from normal process behavior in industrial control systems — boiler pressure, turbine speed, valve actuation — indicative of sensor spoofing or logic manipulation.
ioc_examples: Process variable readings (pressure, temperature, flow) outside normal operating band (>3 sigma from historical baseline)
Modbus write function codes (FC06, FC16) to holding registers from unauthorized source IPs
DNP3 direct operate commands issued outside maintenance windows or from unexpected master station addresses
PLC scan time anomalies: cycle time variance >20% from baseline indicating injected code execution

threat_categories.name: MitM
threat_categories.description: Interception of Modbus/DNP3/IEC 61850 protocol traffic between field devices and control systems.
ioc_examples: Network latency increase of >5ms on SCADA-to-PLC communication paths without infrastructure changes
Modbus/DNP3 response messages from MAC addresses not matching expected device hardware
Duplicate TCP session packets on OT network segments indicating traffic relay/replay
TLS certificate mismatch warnings on encrypted OT communications or unexpected certificate authorities

threat_categories.name: Brute Force
threat_categories.description: Automated credential stuffing against SSH, RDP, and OT management interfaces exposed at the IT/OT boundary.
ioc_examples: SSH authentication failures: >100 failed attempts per minute from single external IP to engineering workstations
RDP login attempts using common username lists (admin, administrator, operator, engineer) from external addresses
Successful authentication following extended failed login sequence (credential stuffing success indicator)
VPN/Citrix gateway logs showing authentication attempts using credentials from known breach databases

threat_categories.name: SQL Injection
threat_categories.description: Database injection attacks targeting historian and asset management databases containing OT telemetry.
ioc_examples: Web application firewall alerts on SQL keywords in HTTP parameters: UNION, SELECT, DROP, INSERT, UPDATE, OR 1=1
Database error messages returned in HTTP responses indicating unsanitized query execution
Unusual query execution times (>10x baseline) indicating complex injected queries like time-based blind SQLi
Database audit logs showing queries returning excessive row counts or accessing credential tables from web app user context

dataset_summary.name: CIC-IDS2017
dataset_summary.description: UNB benchmark dataset with 5 days of realistic network traffic and 8 attack categories across 80+ flow features

dataset_summary.name: ICS Smart Grid Dataset
dataset_summary.description: IEC 60870-104 and IEC 61850 MMS protocol traffic from smart grid testbed with scanning, injection, and DoS attacks
